leads.issues: Homepage, Locations, and Order Online pages all show the literal placeholder title "Your Site Title" in Google search results instead of the business name - visible directly in Google's own listing for the site. This is a website-builder default that was never replaced, so every search result for the business shows no real title at all.
leads.opportunity: Replacing the placeholder title tags across all pages with real, keyword-rich titles (e.g. "Il Valentino Bakery & Café | Dublin") is a same-day fix that immediately restores a professional appearance in search results and recovers click-through rate currently being lost to competitors with proper titles. Expected within 30 days: meaningfully higher click-through from existing search impressions.
leads.email_subject: Your homepage title says 'Your Site Title' on Google
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I was searching for Dublin bakeries today and noticed something on Il Valentino's listing - your homepage, Locations page, and Order Online page all show up on Google with the title "Your Site Title" instead of your actual business name. It looks like a website-builder default that never got replaced.

I put together a quick page showing the fix and what it'd mean for your search visibility:
https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-13-il-valentino-bakery-cafe.html

You can also see how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all, just thought it was worth flagging since it's such a quick fix.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: Google's search result for the business shows only the raw name, address, and phone number with no service description at all - unlike other Toronto florists in the same search results, which show a full descriptive snippet about their arrangements and delivery.
leads.opportunity: Adding a real meta description that describes your arrangements, delivery area, and what makes New Leaf different gives Google something to show searchers beyond your address - immediately closing the gap with competitors who already have a compelling snippet. Expected within 30 days: better click-through on existing searches like "florist Toronto".
